players.notes: AKA "Shotime." CBS rank: consensus 1.02 pick. Auction value ~$53. Dual eligibility (UTIL/SP) makes him uniquely valuable. First-rounder in all formats. Elite five-category contributor with generational talent on both sides of the ball.

AKA "All Rise" / "The Judge." CBS rank: consensus 1.01. Auction value ~$54. Scott White and CBS have him as the top overall pick in 2026 fantasy drafts. First-rounder in outfield tiers. Monster power with 50+ HR upside.

CBS outfield "First-Rounders" tier. Borderline keeper value. Elite power bat with 40+ HR upside. Goes late first / early second round. Schwarber's patience and power combo make him a reliable fantasy anchor per Scott White. Scott White superlative: "This Year's George Springer" (veteran who finds the fountain of youth). Also: "This Year's Jarren Duran" (drafted too high off unrepeatable career year).

CBS outfield "First-Rounders" tier. Now with the Cubs after a blockbuster trade. First-round talent with elite hit tool. Five-category stud when healthy. Potential 30 HR / 20 SB season. Top-10 overall pick in most CBS mocks.
AKA "J-Rod." CBS outfield "First-Rounders" tier. First-round pick. Elite speed and power combo. 30/30 upside. Scott White has him as a first-rounder in outfield tiers. Dynamic five-category player.
AKA "Mr. Smile." CBS has him as a top-tier SS. Elite all-around contributor with 30+ HR, 20+ SB potential. Consistent five-category producer. Named for his famously cheerful personality.

CBS rank: consensus 1.03. Auction value ~$48. Elite SS in the same tier as Elly De La Cruz. Near-lock for 30 HR / 30+ SB. One of the safest five-category players in fantasy. First-round pick in all formats.

AKA "Air Yordan" / "El Grande." Chris Towers' "wildcard" pick. First-round caliber hitter. CBS notes his 2025 absence was mostly a misdiagnosed fractured hand, NOT the recurring knee issues. If healthy, elite power bat with .300 AVG upside. High risk/high reward in rounds 2-3. Scott White superlative: "This Year's Byron Buxton/Trevor Story" (injury-prone veteran who stays healthy and crushes value).
CBS has him going around pick 3.02 in mock drafts. Elite speed remains. Bounce-back candidate after injury-shortened seasons. 20 HR / 30 SB upside. SS depth at a strong position.

CBS SportsLine model flags him as a potential bust — career-lows in AVG (.258) and OPS (.732) last year. Scott White noted Betts and Freeman were the best hitters at the Round 3-4 turn. Still elite talent but declining peripherals raise red flags for early-round investment. Expected ~25 HR.
CBS "Near-Elite" outfield tier. Former MVP with five-category upside when healthy. Injury risk keeps his ADP in the rounds 5-7 range. Still capable of .280+ AVG with 25 HR / 20 SB.

AKA "La Pantera" (The Panther). CBS SportsLine model is high on a bounce-back. Was among the best all-around players in 2023 (38 HR, 80 RBI, 20 SB). Elite tools but durability concerns. High-ceiling, low-floor pick in mid rounds.

Real name: Jasrado Prince Hermis Arrington Chisholm Jr. CBS "Elite" tier at 2B alongside Ketel Marte. Finally hit 30-30 last season. Now with the Yankees. One of only two elite 2B options per CBS position tiers. Premium draft capital at the weakest fantasy position.

CBS SS tier. Elite raw power and speed from the SS position. Swing-and-miss concerns keep him volatile but 30 HR / 20 SB is absolutely in play. High-ceiling mid-round pick. ATC Projections: Most Overvalued.
CBS outfield/3B option. Switch-hitter with 25 HR / 10 SB potential. Solid mid-round value. Consistent producer who doesn't hurt in any category.
CBS "Near-Elite" outfield tier. Speed demon with 30+ SB upside. Plus batting average. Emerged as a legit five-category contributor. Great value in rounds 5-8.

AKA "Freddie Franchise" / "Steady Freddie." CBS "Near-Elite" 1B tier. Being drafted as late as Round 5 — strong value. Hit 14 of 24 HR in second half, slashing .293/.369/.536. Scott White noted he and Mookie Betts were the best hitters available at the Round 3-4 turn in Tout Wars. Expected to provide ~25 HR, 90 RBI, 90 R.
Rookie OF with elite speed. 30+ SB upside with developing power. Five-category contributor potential. Late-round value with high ceiling.
CBS "Near-Elite" outfield tier. Bounce-back candidate with 25+ HR potential. Switch-hitter who can play 1B/OF. Mid-round value. ATC Projections: Most Undervalued.
CBS catcher rank: valued at $24-25 in auction. Second-best catcher behind Cal Raleigh. Capable of challenging for the top spot. Premium bat at catcher — 20+ HR with solid AVG. Draft in rounds 6-8.
CBS rank: 7th among catchers. Prospect making good in 2026 catcher rankings per CBS. Emerging impact bat at the position. Late-round catcher target with upside.
Young SS with the Astros. Gold Glove defender. 15-20 HR with solid defense. More of a real-life asset but provides category balance in fantasy. Late-mid round pick.

Scott White's FEATURED SLEEPER pick. Hit .309/.383/.524 with 20 HR, 17 SB, 16% K rate between Double-A and Triple-A. 5 HR in first 55 MLB at-bats. CBS calls him "one of the preferred sleepers regardless of position." May pick up 2B/3B eligibility. Versatility should assure regular playing time with the Reds. Elite late-round value.
Power/speed outfielder. 30 HR / 15 SB upside. Some batting average concerns but elite counting stats potential. Mid-round outfield option.
Giants OF. Showed power potential in limited time. 20+ HR upside if given full playing time. Late-round flier with some risk.

CBS BREAKOUT candidate. SportsLine projects him as the No. 2 catcher for 2026. Hit .278 / .843 OPS / 31 HR / 91 RBI in 2025 — only Cal Raleigh had more HR among catchers. CBS recommends drafting ahead of Will Smith and Shea Langeliers. Excellent mid-round value. ATC Projections: Most Undervalued.
Cardinals catcher. Showed flashes of a quality bat. Could provide 15 HR with decent AVG if he wins the everyday job. Late-round catcher option.
Braves CF. Five-tool talent with 20 HR / 20 SB ceiling. Elite defense. Mid-round pick with high upside if he stays healthy.
CBS rank: 9th among catchers. Highlighted as a prospect making good alongside Ramirez and Goodman. Cheap late-round catcher option with upside.
Solid OF with elite OBP. Provides runs and walks. 15-20 HR with a strong on-base presence. Better in OBP leagues. Mid-to-late round value.

AKA "The Millville Meteor" / "Kiiiiid" / "King Fish." Former perennial 1.01 pick. Injury concerns have cratered his ADP. If healthy, still an elite five-category player. 30+ HR upside but durability is a massive question mark. Late-round gamble with top-5 ceiling.
Veteran OF with power/speed. 15 HR / 10 SB potential. Deep league option or bench bat. Very late round or undrafted in standard leagues.

Scott White's SLEEPER pick. Signed 4-year, $60M deal with the Blue Jays. CBS calls him "the most straightforward of the NPB imports." Six seasons of 30+ HR in Japan. Supreme bat-to-ball skills with optimized launch angle. ADP outside top 300 — essentially free. Worth a late-round flier in all leagues.
Rockies OF. Power/speed combo at Coors Field. 25 HR / 20 SB upside. Strikeout concerns but elite athleticism. Mid-round outfield target.

CBS "Last Resorts" 1B tier. Overall rank ~202, ADP ~220. CBS says worth drafting in all leagues due to serious power potential. A Taylor Ward-type season (36 HR, 189 combined R+RBI) is possible. Japanese import with the Mets. Very cheap draft price.
1B with consistent 25+ HR power. Solid AVG and counting stats. Reliable mid-round 1B option. Won't win you a league but won't lose it either.

Veteran SS. Returning from injury. If healthy, 20 HR / .275 AVG potential. Risk/reward play in late rounds. Better days may be behind him. Scott White superlative: "This Year's George Springer" (veteran who finds the fountain of youth).
Reds SS/2B. Five-category potential when healthy. 20 HR / 15 SB upside. Injury risk keeps ADP affordable. High-upside mid-round pick.
Utility player with speed. 15+ SB potential. Deep league option with multi-position eligibility. Very late round or free agent pickup.
Nationals 2B. Emerging talent. 20 HR / 15 SB potential as he enters his prime. Could be a value pick in mid-to-late rounds.
Cardinals OF/1B. Showed power growth. 20+ HR potential with solid contact skills. Late-mid round value. Steady contributor.
Rockies SS. Emerging power/speed threat. 20 HR / 15 SB upside at Coors. Young and improving. Mid-round SS value. ATC Projections: Most Undervalued.

CBS "Last Resorts" 1B tier. Posted a .999 OPS in spring training. Popular sleeper/breakout candidate. CBS describes him as a decent 1B option with real limitations, best left for the CI spot. Already paying dividends to those who bought the breakout hype.

CBS 3B tier — near-elite. 3B is described as one of the weakest fantasy positions for 2026. Riley provides 30+ HR consistency with solid RBI totals. Mid-round value at a thin position. Scott White superlative: "This Year's Byron Buxton/Trevor Story" (injury-prone veteran who stays healthy and crushes value).
Veteran 1B/DH. Switch-hitter with 20 HR potential. Deep league option. Bounce-back candidate if he finds the right situation.
CBS: slated for regular playing time vs. righties. Hit 24 HR for the Rockies. Ranked ~176 overall, ~42nd OF per projections. Dealing with oblique tightness in spring. Late-round OF option.

CBS "Last Resorts" OF tier. A pleasant surprise who earned an everyday role. Picked up ABs originally slated for Dylan Crews. CBS says 15 HR / 15 SB is very possible. Handling lefties is his one obstacle. Late-round value pick with the Nationals.

CBS notes: had a power surge in 2025 (31 HR, 83 RBI). Going around pick 119 but projected as the No. 6 2B — ahead of top-100 picks like Hoerner, Semien, and Albies. Scott White's sleeper candidate. Now with the Pirates. Excellent value at the weakest fantasy position.
White Sox 1B/OF. 20+ HR with solid contact. Limited upside but safe floor. Late-mid round option in deeper leagues.

Scott White's BREAKOUT candidate. CBS: "a fair discount at Round 10 in a 12-team league rather than his current ADP of Round 14. Could still perform like a third-rounder." CBS "Fallback Options" at 2B. Bounce-back candidate after injuries. 20 HR / 15 SB upside at a weak position. Scott White superlative: "This Year's Byron Buxton/Trevor Story" (injury-prone veteran who stays healthy and crushes value).

Scott White's BREAKOUT candidate and Chris Towers' WILDCARD pick. CBS: bounce-back candidate at age 35 with "unchanged underlying data from his youth." Ranks just 19th in ADP at a weak 2B position. Could still perform like a third-rounder. Excellent value in rounds 10+. Scott White superlative: "This Year's George Springer" (veteran who finds the fountain of youth).

AKA "Showrrea" / "The Captain." Twins SS. Elite talent with 25 HR potential. Durability has been a concern. When healthy, top-10 SS talent. Mid-round pick with some risk. Scott White superlative: "This Year's George Springer" (veteran who finds the fountain of youth).
CBS "Next-Best Things" tier at 2B alongside Nico Hoerner. Veteran with declining power but strong contact skills. Multi-position eligibility adds flexibility.

AKA "Seags." CBS has him in the SS depth tier. Auction value ~$23 in AL-only. Could provide 30+ HR if you wait ~80 picks past Witt/De La Cruz. Solid batting average with elite power from the SS position. Great value in rounds 4-6. Scott White superlative: "This Year's Byron Buxton/Trevor Story" (injury-prone veteran who stays healthy and crushes value).

CBS rank: SS18 (18th shortstop). Acquired by the Red Sox in the Garrett Crochet trade. 12th overall pick in the draft. First pro season was 'merely so-so' per CBS — considerable power projection from both sides but needs better swing decisions and improved launch angle. Late-round upside play.
CBS 3B tier. Provides 25+ HR with elite defense. Solid mid-round option at a weak fantasy position. Consistent producer with decent counting stats.
Phillies 2B/SS. Solid all-around contributor. 15 HR / 15 SB potential. Won't hurt you in any category. Mid-to-late round middle infielder.
Red Sox utility player. Speed and defensive versatility. 15 HR / 20 SB possible. Multi-position eligibility adds roster flexibility. Late-round pick.
Power 3B. 25+ HR potential. Limited in other categories but provides cheap pop. Late-round option at a thin position.
CBS catcher tier. One of the premier bats at catcher. Provides 20+ HR with solid AVG. Premium position value. Mid-round target.
CBS "Next-Best Things" tier at 2B alongside Jose Altuve. Solid contact hitter with speed. 10 HR / 20 SB potential. Won't hurt your AVG. Provides category balance at a weak position.
Twins 3B/SS. Elite power when healthy. 30+ HR upside in limited games. Massive injury risk — glass cannon profile. Late-round high-ceiling gamble.
Twins OF. Power bat with 25+ HR potential. Swing-and-miss concerns but raw power is legit. Late-round power play.
Veteran 2B/3B. Multi-position eligibility. 15-20 HR potential. Late-round option at weak 2B/3B positions. Better in deeper leagues.
Astros catcher. Premium bat at the position. 20+ HR with solid AVG. One of the better hitting catchers in baseball. Mid-round catcher target.
Veteran utility man. Had a career renaissance. 20 HR / .280 AVG potential. Multi-position eligibility. Late-round value.
Rockies OF prospect. Power/speed tools at Coors Field. 20 HR / 15 SB ceiling. Late-round upside play.
Dodgers 1B/3B. Elite OBP with 25+ HR power. Walk machine. Better in OBP/points leagues. Mid-to-late round value.
Phillies 3B. Contact-first approach. .280+ AVG potential with 15-20 HR. Provides AVG stability at a thin position. Mid-round 3B option. ATC Projections: Most Undervalued.

CBS "Last Resorts" OF tier. Can threaten 20 HR / 30 SB range as Washington's everyday OF. Assuming 130+ games, should easily approach 30 steals. Former No. 2 overall pick. Late-round value with significant upside.
OF with power upside. 20+ HR possible if given regular playing time. Deep league outfield option. Inconsistent but talented.
1B/DH. Platoon bat with power vs. RHP. Deep league option. Very late round or undrafted.

CBS: racked up HR and SB in minors with a high K rate, but it has worked in the majors. Seized everyday job with the Astros in final two weeks of last season. CBS says skepticism remains but he deserves your attention. Late-round speed/power combo.
Blue Jays catcher. Elite contact/OBP at catcher. 15 HR with strong AVG. Better in OBP leagues. Late-round catcher option.
Phillies OF. Solid OBP with developing power. 15 HR / 10 SB potential. Better in OBP formats. Late-round outfield pick.
CBS: not prominently featured in 2B tiers. Valued at ~$2 in fantasy. Traded from Blue Jays to Red Sox. Getting reps at 3B and 2B in spring. Very late round or undrafted.
Dodgers utility. Contact-oriented bat. Multi-position eligibility. Deep league option with some AVG upside.
Yankees SS. 15 HR / 20 SB potential. Developing bat with elite speed. Better in stolen base leagues. Mid-to-late round SS option.
Utility infielder. Contact-oriented. Deep league pickup only. Limited counting stats upside.
Blue Jays 3B/OF. 20 HR potential with developing bat. Multi-position eligibility. Late-round CI/OF option.

CBS "Fallback Options" catcher tier. Arrived in September with 95.4 mph avg exit velocity and 13% walk rate. CBS says a possible superstar outcome is on the table. Heir to Salvador Perez in KC. Late-round catcher with high ceiling.
Yankees catcher. Emerging bat behind the plate. 15-20 HR with solid OBP. Young and improving. Late-round catcher value.
Rays OF. Power/speed combo. 20 HR / 25 SB upside. Solid five-category contributor. Mid-to-late round OF target.
Phillies catcher. Premium bat at the position when healthy. 15-20 HR with speed rare for a catcher. Age and injury concerns lower his ADP. Late-mid round catcher.
CBS "Leftovers" at 2B. Has 2B eligibility but primarily plays 3B. Playing time could be impacted by Kevin McGonigle's arrival. Not a major breakout candidate per CBS. Late-round 2B/3B option.
Orioles OF. Speed-first profile. 15 HR / 25 SB potential. AVG can be volatile. Late-round speed source.
Rangers 3B. 25+ HR potential when healthy. Injury concerns keep his ADP in check. Good value at a thin 3B position. Mid-to-late round pick.
Veteran OF. .280+ AVG with 20 HR potential. Solid floor player. Late-round outfield option.
OF with speed and defense. 15 HR / 15 SB potential. Deep league option or bench outfielder.

AKA "Rooster" / "Clutchman." CBS: valued at only $5 for 2026. Ranked OUTSIDE the elite catcher tier. Disappointing fantasy production relative to real-life value. Late-round catcher — buy low candidate if you believe in a bounce-back.
Mariners SS. Solid OBP with limited power. Better in OBP leagues. Late-round middle infield option. Won't hurt your AVG.
Twins catcher. 20+ HR potential at the position. Solid power bat for a catcher. Late-round option behind the top tier.
Rockies 3B. 25 HR at Coors Field. Solid power at a thin position. Mid-to-late round 3B target.
Angels 1B. Elite plate discipline. More walks than power. Better in OBP leagues. Late-round 1B option.
Cardinals OF. Solid OBP and contact. 15 HR / 10 SB potential. Better in OBP formats. Late-round pick.
Veteran OF. Contact hitter with .275+ AVG potential. Limited power and speed. Deep league outfield option.
Giants OF. 20 HR with solid OBP. Platoon concerns. Better vs. RHP. Late-round outfield option.
Reds/Mariners OF. Emerging bat with 15-20 HR potential. Late-round flier. Scott White superlative: "This Year's Geraldo Perdomo" (drafted past pick 400 who returns top-50 value).
Cardinals 2B/3B. 25+ HR power potential. High strikeout rate limits AVG. Power bat at weak positions. Late-round gamble.
Mets 3B/1B. 30+ HR power potential. Emerging as a legit power threat. Mid-round value at a thin 3B position.
Mets 3B. Still has prospect pedigree but has struggled at the MLB level. Upside play only. Very late round or undrafted.
Padres utility. Multi-position eligibility. 15-20 HR with solid AVG. Steady contributor. Late-round value with flexibility.
Mets 2B/OF. Contact-first bat. .280+ AVG potential. Limited power. Better in AVG leagues. Late-round 2B option.
Brewers SS/3B. Emerging talent. 15 HR / 10 SB potential. Multi-position eligibility. Late-round option.
Guardians 2B. 15 HR / 20 SB potential. Strong defender. Inconsistent bat. Mid-to-late round 2B option.
AKA "Goldy." CBS Near-Elite 1B tier in recent years but aging. Veteran 1B. Power and OBP declining with age. 20 HR potential. Late-round 1B option. Buy low if he lands in a good lineup.

Orioles 2B/SS. Top prospect pedigree. Five-category upside if he puts it all together. Struggled in initial MLB stints. High-ceiling late-round pick. ATC Projections: Most Overvalued (projection systems see regression, not growth).
Reds 2B. Former ROY. 15-20 HR with solid OBP. Injury prone. Late-round 2B option.
Power OF. 30+ HR upside but never stays healthy. Classic glass cannon. Late-round power gamble.
Blue Jays 1B/2B. OBP-focused bat. 15 HR with strong walk rate. Better in OBP leagues. Late-round option.
White Sox 1B/OF. Platoon bat. 15 HR vs. RHP. Deep league only.
Pirates 3B. Contact-oriented. 10-15 HR potential. Deep league option at a thin position.
Twins utility. Multi-position eligibility. 15 HR / 15 SB potential. Late-round flexibility pick.
Orioles 2B/3B. 20+ HR potential. Multi-position eligibility at weak positions. Mid-to-late round value.

Chris Towers' BREAKOUT pick. No. 6 overall pick from 2024 draft. Dominated minors with 170 wRC+. CBS: top-of-the-spectrum raw power with 77.4 mph bat speed (better than Schwarber). Rookie season disappointed (.239 wOBA) but .321 xwOBA suggests he wasn't far off. Needs to lift the ball more. Cheap look at elite tools. Late-round high-upside gamble with the Royals.
Veteran 3B. Traded by the Cardinals. 20+ HR with strong defense. Declining production but still solid. Late-round 3B option. CBS has not prioritized him in 2026 tiers.

CBS "Fallback Options" at C. CBS: more of a real-life asset than fantasy one. Power profile projects as a teen-homer guy. Will share the position with the White Sox. Late-round catcher with limited upside.
Astros OF. Platoon outfielder. 15 HR potential vs. LHP. Deep league only.
Orioles/Marlins 2B. Young infielder with some pop. Deep league option.

CBS "Last Resorts" at 1B. DH-only to start 2026. The 6'7" prospect delivered a 95.7 mph avg exit velocity at Triple-A — described as "Judgian" by CBS. Needs to make more contact and may have been rushed. All homers with low AVG and no speed. Pure power profile. Late-round gamble.
OF. 15-20 HR potential. Injury concerns limit his upside. Late-round OF depth.
Diamondbacks 1B/OF. Limited fantasy upside. Deep league option only.
Rangers utility. Multi-position eligibility. Contact bat with some speed. Deep league flexibility pick.
CBS: elite speed with 93 SB since start of 2024, trailing only Elly De La Cruz. Late-round source of steals. If you need SB, this is your guy. Very cheap price for elite speed.
Angels catcher. 20 HR potential at the position. Developing bat. Mid-to-late round catcher option.
Mets catcher. Power bat at the position. 25+ HR potential. Premium catcher when healthy. Mid-round target.
Utility infielder. Deep league only. Limited fantasy value.
Cubs 3B. 20+ HR with elite plate discipline. Strong OBP. Late-round 3B value, better in OBP leagues.
Twins OF. Power potential. 20 HR upside if healthy. Inconsistent playing time. Late-round flier.
Prospect OF. Limited MLB track record. Dynasty asset more than redraft value. Very late round or undrafted.
Veteran OF/DH. 25+ HR power. Streaky but can carry a team for stretches. Late-round power bat.
Reds OF. Speed and contact. 15 HR / 20 SB potential. Injury concerns. Late-round speed/contact option.
Cardinals OF. Elite speed. 30+ SB upside. Limited power. Late-round speed specialist.
Young prospect. Limited MLB exposure. Dynasty stash more than redraft value.
Cardinals OF/3B. Elite prospect pedigree. 25 HR potential if it clicks. Has struggled at the MLB level. Late-round upside play — buy low on the talent.
Rangers OF. Young five-tool prospect. 15 HR / 15 SB potential. Needs more seasoning. Late-round pick with high ceiling.
Listed among Scott White's BREAKOUT picks at catcher. D-backs C. Premium bat at the position. 15+ HR with strong AVG. Emerging as a top-5 catcher. Mid-round catcher target.
Twins SS/3B prospect. Solid all-around bat. 15 HR potential. Could contribute across categories. Late-round option.
Veteran OF/DH. Platoon power bat. 25+ HR vs. RHP. Deep league option or platoon specialist.
Prospect SS. No prominent CBS 2026 coverage found. Dynasty prospect with tools. Very late round or undrafted in redraft.
Brewers OF. Contact-first approach. 10 HR / 15 SB potential. Won't hurt your AVG. Deep league OF option.
Guardians catcher. Power potential at the position. 15-20 HR upside. Late-round catcher option.
Twins 2B. Elite OBP with 20 HR power. High walk rate. Better in OBP leagues. Late-round 2B value.
Phillies OF. 20+ HR with solid AVG. Consistent producer. Late-round outfield depth.
CBS: 'fringier option' at 2B. Could benefit from Coors Field. Very late round or undrafted. Deep league speed option.
CBS "Last Resorts" at C. Positioned as a low-end No. 2 catcher rather than fringe top-12. Late-round catcher depth.
CBS "Leftovers" at 2B. First full MLB season in 2025: 22 HR, 75 RBI, .264 AVG with impressive 10.4% barrel rate and strong contact rates. Late-round 2B with some pop.

CBS "Last Resorts" OF tier. CBS calls him "the most popular cheap speed flyer for 2026 drafts." Slashed .334/.411/.452 with 7 HR and 46 SB at Triple-A as a 21-year-old. Expected to open as Phillies' primary CF. Elite speed. Late-round SB source with AVG upside.

CBS: three years in and never been healthy (sports hernia, broken hamate, thrice-broken foot). Productive when healthy. Made MLB debut in playoffs. Seems assured of Opening Day lineup if he gets through spring. High risk/high reward with the Guardians.
Reds C/1B. Contact hitter with 15 HR potential. Injury prone. Late-round catcher or CI option.
Prospect. Limited MLB data. Deep league or dynasty stash only.
Tigers OF. Speed and defense. 15 HR / 15 SB potential. Developing bat. Late-round OF option.

Scott White's BREAKOUT 2.0 pick. Five-category potential with elite plate discipline. Cardinals cleared a path after trading Arenado and Donovan. Will be eligible at SS initially, likely settles at 2B — a position starved for high-end talent. CBS: "could emerge as a top-five 2B in either roto or points as soon as this year." Excellent late-round value.
CBS "Leftovers" at 2B. Had an impressive rookie debut in 2025 with strong 2B and SS play. Late-round 2B option with the White Sox.

CBS: ranked 34th in Top 100 Prospects. Acquired in the Edward Cabrera deal with the Marlins. Power has long been his selling point. All but ensured an Opening Day lineup spot. Three-true-outcomes profile who grew into his power. Late-round power OF upside.

CBS: top prospect at catcher. Elite exit velocity (94.2 mph avg, 115.9 mph max at Triple-A). CBS says a "Kyle Schwarber-like outcome is on the table." Expected to qualify at C but DH more regularly. Projects ~20 HR / .240-.250 AVG. Late-round catcher with massive upside.
Guardians SS/3B. Utility option. Limited fantasy upside. Deep league only.
Prospect C. Limited MLB track record. Dynasty stash more than redraft value.
Cardinals utility. Multi-position eligibility. 10 HR / 25 SB potential. Elite speed. Late-round flexibility and SB source.

CBS: potentially the No. 1 overall prospect. Baseball America gave him an 80-grade hit tool. CBS calls him a "potential five-category monster." Hit .305 with 19 HR and 10 SB in 88 games. Tigers management hasn't downplayed him claiming everyday job despite not advancing past Double-A. "Superlative bat-to-ball skills should make for a smooth transition." Dynasty and redraft darling.
Guardians SS/2B. Contact-oriented middle infielder. Limited power. Deep league option.
Tigers OF/2B. Speed option. 15+ SB potential. Deep league pick.
Veteran catcher. Declining production. Deep league backup catcher only.
Pirates 3B. Elite defense, limited offense. 10-15 HR. Doesn't help in fantasy beyond AVG. Deep league 3B.
Cubs C prospect. Young bat with upside. 15 HR potential. Late-round dynasty-crossover catcher option.
Angels utility. Multi-position eligibility. 15 HR / 10 SB. Late-round flexibility.
Braves catcher. 20+ HR at the position. Power bat behind the plate. Mid-to-late round catcher option.
Rangers 2B/SS. Ronald Acuña Jr.'s brother. Speed-first profile. 20+ SB potential. Late-round speed play.
Giants catcher. Developing bat. 15 HR potential with strong defense. Late-round catcher option.
Veteran catcher. Backup/platoon role. Deep league only.
CBS "Leftovers" at C. With Kyle Teel also on the White Sox, CBS says it's hard to see Quero becoming the starter. Very limited fantasy appeal. Undrafted in most formats.
Royals catcher. Backup role behind Perez/Jensen. Deep league only.
Elite speed specialist with 40+ SB upside. Limited power but elite stolen base contributor. Late-round speed source.
Contact-first MI with speed. 25+ SB potential with solid AVG. Limited power. Late-round MI option at a thin position.

SP #19, Tier 4. Backed up 2024 Cy Young w/ another AGA season until fractured rib (2+ months). Slider/four-seam combo elite. Sinker to LHB had 5.94 PLV. Volume ceiling far lower than peers — injury risk caps value in 12+teamers.

SP #9, Tier 2. Elite four-seamer velocity jumped to 99+ mph (PLV #1 among SP) w/ improved slider feel. Groin strains limited 2025 following 150 IP in 2024. Elite 14.7% SwStr% and 30%+ K potential. Injury history caps volume floor vs. top-tier peers; best valued in shallow leagues with strong wire replacements.

SP #64, Tier 13 (IL Stash). Mild TOS diagnosis — ETA ~June 1. Core four IL stash w/ Cole, Rodon, Jones. Won't be detrimental once back. Announced retirement after 2027 — motivated to give everything these final two years.

SP #8, Tier 2. Grade 1 forearm strain cost ~6 weeks. 3.43 ERA, 1.03 WHIP. Elite 7.6 ft extension. Slider location poor (86th %ile mid-zone) but still effective via tunneling. Added splitter as #3 pitch. Another 200 IP of elite production expected.

SP #13, Tier 3. Four-seamer elite via 99th percentile HAVAA and extension despite HR risk (16 in 2025). Increased sinker usage (15%+) helping to mitigate longball risk to RHB. Reliable 200-K upside w/ elite WHIP floor. 2024 teres strain a non-factor; back stiffness won't delay Opening Day. High-floor SP2 with untapped secondary upside.

SP #54, Tier 10. Moving to Boston. 40%+ ICR across pitches to LHB — concerning. Sub-70% LOB rate two straight years. Sweeper still landing well. Gains Win potential w/ Red Sox. Banking on Boston coaching to bring necessary tweaks. Scott White superlative: "This Year's Aaron Nola" (veteran pitcher who completely falls off a cliff).

SP #70. Been on Castillo train since debut. Stuff grades remain strong but results inconsistent. Changeup/sinker combo can dominate. Seattle run support remains concern for Wins. Scott White superlative: "This Year's Aaron Nola" (veteran pitcher who completely falls off a cliff).
RP #2 (Astros). Elite closer entering prime. Nasty slider/four-seam combo. 35+ SV+H projection. One of the most reliable closers in baseball. Must-roster in all formats.
SP #11. Back on mound after TJS. Slider/sweeper combo elite. Massive Win potential w/ Dodgers lineup. Volume the question — likely managed carefully. When pitching, expect elite ratios and K rates.

RP (Yankees). Shook off early demotion to finish top-10 reliever. 2.47 SIERA, 26.7% K-BB, 34.3% CSW. Key fix: threw FB less (-12%), improved curveball command (low loc +18.5%). Zone rate 49.1% — best since 2021.
SP #24. TJS return went well — showed he's capable. Slider remains dominant offering. Volume managed by Rays. High-end stuff when healthy, durability the ongoing concern.

SP #65. Bone spur procedure, mid-May return (likely drifting later). Talent never the question — command is. Changeup to RHB inconsistent even when healthy. IL-start risks: timeline drift, disrupted feel. Value at pick 200, overpay much earlier.

RP (Padres). Another dominant campaign — 2.55 SIERA, 35.5% K, 18.1% SwStr, 5.24 PLV. Four-seam/slider combo elite (98 MPH, 18.8" iVB, 70.2% high loc). Splitter bat-missing weapon. Setup role locked w/ Adam on IL.

SP #78. Biggest bust of 2025 per PitcherList. Stuff still flashes — slider is legit. Command and consistency fell off. Buy-low candidate if you believe in talent. Needs to find strike zone more reliably.

RP #1. Finished w/ 2.09 SIERA, 38.3% K, 20.7% SwStr, 117 Stuff+ over final 50.1 IP. Bad luck inflated ERA — underlying stuff unchanged from previous 5 seasons. Signed w/ Mets, motivated to prove doubters wrong. Elite closer.

RP (Phillies). Great deadline add, solidified closer role. 2.59 xFIP, 21.3% K-BB, 125 Stuff+. More safe/floor than ceiling now — K rate declining yearly. 100th %ile groundball rate, just 7 HR over 2 seasons. Dependable closer.
SP #11. Back from elbow issues. Elite stuff when healthy — overpowering fastball/curveball combo. With Dodgers. Volume the question. High ceiling but injury risk limits floor.
SP #44. We KNOW what he does. Elite stuff, maddening inconsistency. With Dodgers. Volume remains the concern. When locked in, as dominant as anyone. Draft with eyes open about floor.

RP (Brewers). 30+ saves in under 50 IP — dominant. 31.3% K, 33.7% CSW, 128 Stuff+, 5.48 PLV. FB/curveball combo one of best per PLV. Health concern — hasn't stayed healthy full season. FB location needs work (42% hiLoc).

RP (White Sox). Impressive debut despite rough ratios — 2.54 xFIP, 34.4% K. FB sat 98.7 MPH w/ 7.4 ft extension. Cutter 95.3 MPH. Curveball best pitch (40% CSW). Still SP candidate long-term. Stuff plays up in pen.

RP (Blue Jays). Poor HR luck — 15 HR after 14 combined over previous 3 seasons. Stuff intact (29.3% K, 16.1% SwStr). Four-seam responsible for 10 HR on 18 hits. Won't allow 15 HR again — expect much better ERA in 2026.

RP (Astros). Abreu/Hader duo one of best in baseball. Elite 2025 — 35.5% K, 19.1% SwStr. Slider truly unique (12.2" break, 2918 rpm, 21.7% SwStr + 19.1% CSR). Top setup man, hits FA 2027 w/ closer interest.

SP #101. Dominated in reliever role — 30% K, 16% SwStr, 5.45 PLV. FB jumped to 96+ MPH but expect 2 ticks less as starter. Slider command still spotty, 75% ICR to LHB on four-seam. HIPSTER flag — same command issues from SP days.
SP #49. In on Nola for 2026. Proven durability and quality. Curveball remains elite offering. Steady floor with occasional upside. Reliable SP2/SP3 target.
SP #89. Not as sold as others on Priester. With Milwaukee now. Stuff okay but not elite. Deeper league target.
RP (Cardinals). Elite closer when healthy. Stuff dominant. FB/slider combo overpowers hitters. Save source locked. One of safest RP1 options. Draft with confidence.
SP #60. Excited for Musgrove return to mound. Stuff has always played. Health is everything — when right, legitimate top-30 arm. Slider/cutter mix is filthy.

RP (Rays). Emerging arm in Tampa bullpen. Stuff grades well. High-leverage potential. Worth monitoring in deeper leagues. Scott White superlative: "This Year's Emilio Pagan" (reliever who takes over an ambiguous bullpen for a huge season).
SP (D-backs). Returning from injury. When healthy, legitimate top-30 arm. Stuff plays — elite command with solid K rates. Buy-low candidate if health cooperates.

SP #63, Tier 13 (IL Stash). TJS return ~June. Slider put-away dropped 27%→12% — deployment issue, not stuff. Cutter addition gives legit count pitch. 7% walk rate was anomalous (arm likely hurt). Top IL stash target.
RP (Dodgers). Elite lefty reliever. Slider dominates. High-leverage role locked. One of the better LHP relievers in baseball. Strong SV+HLD value.

RP (Rays). Crazy unlucky 2025 — worst ratios of reliever career but 2.21 xFIP, 27.6% K-BB, 36% CSW, 5.42 PLV. Sweeper elite (88 MPH, 3000+ rpm). Changeup nasty (27% SwStr). Four-seam command needs work (45% hiLoc). High-leverage role.
SP #97. Silly to stick Grayson this low. With Angels now. Talent undeniable. Needs to put it together for full season. Buy-low if possible.
RP. Veteran w/ closing experience. Stuff can still miss bats in short stints. Role security the concern — committee situations limit value. Best in SV+HLD formats.
SP #83. Should be drafting Ponce. Won't cost you much. With Toronto. Stuff plays. Sneaky value pick in middle rounds.
RP. Young arm with intriguing stuff. Developing reliever profile. Worth monitoring for potential breakout. Deep league stash.
RP (Rays). Part of Tampa's bullpen mix. Stuff plays. Contributing in high-leverage spots. Worth rostering in SV+HLD formats.
SP #46. Traded to Cubs — fun landing spot. Stuff is electric. Health and command the two dragons to slay. When right, elite ceiling. Classic high-risk/high-reward arm.
RP (Brewers). Very similar numbers to Megill (30.2% K, 33.5% CSW, 117 Stuff+). Touches triple digits. Pushes Megill or takes over if Megill misses time. High upside.
RP. Experienced arm, proven track record. Can eat innings. Stuff still plays. Versatile — start or relieve. Solid floor in SV+HLD.
RP. Middle-relief arm with decent metrics. Stuff solid if unspectacular. Deeper league value only. Role security uncertain.
SP #71. Poster child for consistency. With Arizona. Reliable arm year after year. Won't win you the league but won't lose it. Steady SP3.
SP #32. Perfect example of pitcher who found it. With Baltimore now. Stuff plays up. Legitimate breakout candidate in great situation. Should be rostered in all formats.
RP (Red Sox). Stuff has always been elite. Health constant concern. When available, dominant metrics. Versatile — pen or rotation. High upside if healthy.
SP #58. 2025 wasn't great. Strong end to season though. With Baltimore now. Stuff still flashes. High ceiling if health cooperates.

SP #118 (Mets). Lost velocity tick in each of last 2 seasons. Couldn't replicate 2024 2nd-half magic. Changeup shelved for FB/sweepers — not getting it done. No 6-frame games. Don't draft expecting rebound.

SP #121 (Angels). 97+ MPH sinker w/ true sink — ~70% groundball rate. Curveball CSW darling. But 11% walk rate, volatile command. Slider could catalyze breakout (21% SwStr). Angels' worst-in-MLB infield defense kills value.
SP #47. Sounds wild but actually underrated. Stuff plays in Cincinnati. Good K rates. Developing arm with room for growth. Should be drafted in all 12+ team leagues.
RP. Contributing in bullpen role. Stuff decent. Deeper league value in SV+HLD.
SP #82. Bad year in 2025. Served plenty of HRs. Stuff still solid but needs to limit damage. Buy-low window if he corrects HR issues.
RP. Veteran reliever w/ closing experience. Health has been concern. When right, solid late-inning arm. SV+HLD value.
SP #67. Is he healthy? Update: No. With Atlanta. When healthy, stuff is elite. Injury concerns cloud outlook. High risk/high reward.
RP. Lefty with solid metrics. Stuff plays in short stints. Role-dependent value. SV+HLD formats best fit.
RP. Lefty reliever with solid hold potential. Effective if unflashy. Committee role caps ceiling. SV+HLD value.
RP (Giants). Dominant metrics in setup role. Stuff grades very well. High-leverage locked. Strong K rates. One of better non-closer relievers.
RP. Elite stuff — slider is a weapon. Closing experience. K rates excellent. Save source potential. One of premier LHP closers available. Draft as RP1.
RP. Veteran setup man with solid metrics. Consistent hold producer. SV+HLD value, less useful in saves-only.
SP #91. Wonderful surprise for White Sox. Stuff played well. Young arm with developing arsenal. Worth rostering in 12+ team leagues.

SP #145 (Cubs). Wide arsenal, throws strikes at will. Sub-20% K rate but solid ratios. New kick-change incredible (20% SwStr to LHB). Sub-4.00 ERA realistic, 65%+ QS rate. Safer than unknown HIPSTERs.
RP. Power arm with swing-and-miss stuff. High K rates. Command inconsistent. Boom-or-bust profile. SV+HLD upside in deeper leagues.
SP #75. One simple reason to like Henderson: elite four-seamer. Brewers managing innings after injury scare. Once given full rotation spot, high upside. FB too good to keep down long.
RP (Padres). 2025 All-Star, 13 wins — top-50 overall Yahoo player. Can't expect double-digit wins again but dominant in holds leagues. Elite stuff.

SP #199 (Brewers). Cutter/sinker/four-seam mix had unsustainable 25% K rate. Putaway rates on cutter/four-seam likely won't hold. Struggles vs LHB — no proper breaker or changeup. K rate should fall to ~20%.
SP #59. Frustrated Nelson didn't take bigger step. Stuff plays in Arizona. Room for growth in command. Should be rostered and started in most formats.

SP #165 (Marlins). Didn't pitch 2025 (TJS/internal brace). Slider primary weapon, cutter complements beautifully. Notable absence: quality fastball. Path through sub-30% heater usage. Streaming upside if healthy.
SP #68. TJS comeback with Toronto. Stuff historically elite. Health is the only question. When right, top-20 arm.
SP #66. Already on 60-day IL — earliest return May 25th. Internal Brace UCL surgery. Stuff is electric. With Pittsburgh. High ceiling, IL stash candidate.
RP. Power arm with solid metrics. Contributing in high-leverage situations. SV+HLD value in deeper formats.
SP #56. Cameron's 2025 success surprised some. With Kansas City. Stuff plays. Legitimate rotation piece. Should be rostered in 12+ team leagues.
RP (Giants). Submarine specialist. Zero K upside, just gets outs. Not recommended in SV+HLD per PitcherList.

SP #143 (D-backs). Mess of a season. Poor four-seam, average sinker. Added cutter 2nd half — 70%+ strikes to LHB. Still needs better changeup. Long leash, can go 9 on best days. Waiver pickup if everything clicks.

SP #139 (Mets). HIPSTER. SWATCH potential w/ FB up, sinkers away, whiffable slider (27% SwStr but 40% ICR). 94th %ile extension. Changeup lacks SwStr (13%). WHIP routinely 1.30+. Streamer only, not season-long hold.

SP #135 (Tigers). Four-seam deadzone movement, batters hunt it at 95 MPH. Sinker earns 80% strikes. Splitter at 30%+ usage gets destroyed. Needs more slider (31% ICR) and less four-seam. Low 6% walk rate. Late-round flier on opportunity.
RP. Lefty reliever with decent metrics. Role-dependent value. Deeper league SV+HLD only.
RP (Phillies). Young arm with elite stuff. High-leverage potential. Developing closer profile. Worth rostering in all SV+HLD formats.
SP #36. Keep oscillating on Yesavage. With Toronto. Stuff is legit. High ceiling arm. Should be drafted in all formats as SP3/SP4 target.

SP #142 (Reds). 30 starts of Toby goodness + 2 disasters. Slider figuring out 60%+ strikes vs RHB. Sinker poor vs LHB (55%+ ICR). GB rate plummeted to 43%. Decent 15-teamer floor, lacks 12-teamer ceiling.
RP. Experienced LHP reliever. Steady metrics. Hold producer. SV+HLD value. Reliable in short stints.
RP (Padres). Starting on IL. When healthy, elite closer-caliber stuff. Curveball dominant. Will return to high-leverage role. Worth stashing early.
RP (Rays). Part of Tampa bullpen mix. Stuff decent. Deeper league value.
RP. Contributing in bullpen. Developing arm. Deeper league consideration only.
RP. Emerging reliever with intriguing stuff. Worth monitoring. Deeper league stash potential.
RP (Marlins). Young lefty with good metrics. Developing closer profile. Worth rostering in SV+HLD.
RP. Solid bullpen arm. Role-dependent value. SV+HLD consideration in deeper leagues.
RP. Power arm with swing-and-miss potential. Developing reliever. Deep league stash.
SP #77. Does Ashcraft break the Huascar Rule? Stuff is legit for Pittsburgh. Young arm with high ceiling. Durability and development are the unknowns.
RP. Veteran arm. Stuff still plays in short stints. Hold producer. SV+HLD value in deeper formats.
RP (Mariners). Electric stuff — FB sits 97+. TJS recovery. When healthy, elite swing-and-miss. High-leverage ceiling. Worth stashing for upside.
RP. Versatile arm who can start or relieve. Stuff solid. Worth monitoring in deeper formats.
RP. Closing experience — saved Yankee season in 2024 playoffs. Stuff solid but not elite. Role security matters. SV+HLD value.
SP #190 (Orioles). [PitcherList blurb in progress at time of publication — no detailed notes available.]

SP #138 (Mets). Unique sinker-first starter — 60% GB rate. Secondaries don't earn enough strikes. Sweeper SwStr fell 20%→11%. WHIP will balloon to 1.25+ from GB BABIP + walks. Deep league only, chasing sub-4.00 ERA.
RP. Power arm with decent metrics. Deeper league SV+HLD value.
RP. Veteran arm. Stuff still effective in short stints. Hold producer. SV+HLD consideration in deeper leagues.
SP #94. All eyes on Matthews. With Minnesota. Legit stuff. Developing arm w/ fantasy-relevant ceiling. Should be stashed in 12+ team leagues.
Not ranked in provided PitcherList article portions (likely ranked 200+).
RP. Closer-caliber stuff. Power FB/slider combo. When healthy, elite late-inning arm. Health ongoing concern. High upside if available.
SP #147 (Royals). Changeup-first RHP. Sub-4.00 ERA, sub-1.25 WHIP in each of last 4 seasons. Two-tick changeup velo drop hurt LHB dominance. KC dimensions may push ERA above 4.00. AL-Only sleeper.
RP. Veteran LHP. TJS comeback. Stuff has always been solid. Health is massive question. Deep league flier.

SP #108 (Guardians). Command artist — FB/changeup separation to RHB is SWATCH dream. Slider sits away to LHB, sinkers jam inside. Changeup ~20% SwStr. Preferred over Allen/Cantillo. Beautiful strikezone plots. Exciting SWATCH potential.

SP #102. Completely lost season — shoulder surgery shortly after March start. Expected to stretch out as starter. Slider gets whiffs, curve lovely companion vs LHB. Free flier last round. ReyLó could surprise again like 2024.
RP (Giants). Closing experience but lost role. Slider nasty. Command the issue — walks too many. Boom-or-bust. Upside is elite closer if he finds zone.

SP #191 (Athletics). Changeup is fantastic but no supporting cast. Slider has horrific 14% putaway to LHB. No sinker. Four-seam PLV sub-20th %ile. Sacré Verde home park. Too volatile — don't start at home.
SP #99. Electric summer presence dissipated in fall. With Athletics. Young arm with developing stuff. Deep league stash.
RP. Veteran closer with solid track record. Curveball/slider mix effective. Save source potential.

RP. Dominant closer when on. Elite splitter. Health history concerning. When available, one of best closers in baseball. Scott White superlative: "This Year's Emilio Pagan" (reliever who takes over an ambiguous bullpen for a huge season).
SP #81. Stuff is intriguing with Cleveland. Young lefty with developing arsenal. Fantasy-relevant ceiling but needs reps. Watch for spring reports.
SP #92. Is this the year? Stuff speaks to superstar potential. With Texas. Needs to put it together at MLB level. Upside play in all formats.
RP (Phillies). Power LHP with elite velocity. Stuff overpowers hitters. Command can be wild. High-leverage role. Boom-or-bust.
SP #95. Going to see Burrows on a lot of sleeper lists. With Houston. Stuff plays. Great pitching org. Stash candidate.

SP #140 (Cardinals). Ace by default. 94 MPH four-seam fluctuates 92-95. Sinker 1st %ile inside loc to LHB. Slider/cutter/curve solid vs LHB. Changeup not SWATCH weapon (8th %ile drop). Needs peak command to be Toby.

SP #137 (Tigers). Stellar 2nd half at age 42 — 2.60 ERA, 1.20 WHIP, 23% K in final 13 games. FB/slider/curveball core. Struggled vs LHB. Volume arm for deeper formats. 15-teamer Toby, not 12-teamer material.
RP. Lefty reliever with solid metrics. Contributing in bullpen. SV+HLD value.

SP #160 (Orioles). Perfect streamer vs poor offenses. FB 92/93 MPH w/ solid vert, located up top incessantly. Slider befuddled batters (29% ICR, 15% SwStr, 72% strikes). Elite precision compensates for lack of pizazz. Wire pickup.
RP. Emerging arm with promising metrics. Worth monitoring. Deeper league consideration.

SP #166 (Marlins). Slider genuinely elite — 89-90 MPH w/ 86 MPH movement in two planes. FB velocity spike (95+) came at physical cost: hip pain. Everything depends on hip. Temper expectations until spring tells more.
RP. Experienced closer. Stuff solid if unspectacular. Save source in right situation. Steady floor.
SP #88. Possibly re-signing somewhere. Age and durability real concerns. Still Mad Max competitiveness. Deep league flier only.
Not ranked in provided PitcherList article portions (likely ranked 200+).
RP. Power arm with K potential. Stuff flashes elite. Command inconsistent. SV+HLD upside when locked in.

SP #151 (Cubs, IL Stash). TJS recovery, hope for All-Star break return. Four-seam w/ cut action elite upstairs. Master of soft contact, plays into Cubs D. SP2/3 when healthy. Last-round IL stash, monitor mid-season.
RP. Young arm with elite stuff. High K rates. Developing closer profile. Command the hurdle. High upside stash.
RP. Veteran LHP closer. Steady metrics. Save source when in role. Lefty specialist value. SV+HLD reliable.
SP #80. Fantastic in 2023 debut. Hasn't stayed healthy since. Ghost Fork is elite pitch. If healthy, top-30 arm. Massive 'if' at this point.
RP (D-backs). Co-closer. Just 25.2 IP in 2025, battled injuries. 24% K, 13.2% SwStr. Poor BABIP luck (.360 vs .258 xBABIP). Velocity dip concerning (1 MPH FB, 2.5 MPH slider). Needs health first.
SP #194 (Nationals). Signed after 3 seasons in Japan. Craft southpaw, discount SWATCH. Sweeper/cutter favored to RHB. Low-90s velocity. Will get volume for Nats. Toby lurking if command is there.
Not ranked in provided PitcherList article portions (likely ranked 200+).
SP #96. Wild ride last year. Stuff plays with Atlanta. Young arm with developing arsenal. Fantasy-relevant in deeper leagues.
Not ranked in provided PitcherList article portions (likely ranked 200+).
Not ranked in provided PitcherList article portions (likely ranked 200+).

SP #149 (Athletics). Workload is the draw — starts every 5 days, 90+ pitch leash. But no stellar pitch. 96 MPH heaters get strikes not whiffs (50% ICR on four-seam). Sacré Verde home park. AL-Only or road streamer.

SP #195 (Cardinals). Sinker-first command arm — 40% usage to RHB, sub 3" vert (legit sinker), 24% ICR at ~80% strikes. Needs sweeper improvements to push K rate toward 20%. Streamer vs poor lineups in 12-teamers.
SP #110 (FA). No job yet. When cruising in 2025, had 94 MPH w/ whiffable slider and changeup, could go 6 IP. Right elbow irritation ended season. In-season pickup only. Don't draft currently.

SP #141 (Guardians). Four-seam 50%+ ICR to both sides. Curveball (30% putaway to LHB) and slider (20% SwStr) show hope for ~22% K. But fastballs too hittable. 85% LOB rate won't hold. AL-Only leagues only.
SP #84. Late start to season. With Boston. Stuff is solid. Changeup remains plus offering. Steady arm when healthy. SP3/SP4 range.
SP #100. Last year was a journey. With Yankees. Initially rough but found footing. Developing arm with room for growth. Deep league target with upside.
SP #52. May scare some on volume but stuff is real. With Yankees. Legitimate rotation piece. Should be rostered in all 12+ team formats.
SP #86. Became a bit of a HIPSTER last season. With Giants. Stuff flashes but consistency lacking. Deeper league target.
Not ranked in provided PitcherList article portions (likely ranked 200+).
Not ranked in provided PitcherList article portions (likely ranked 200+).

SP #136 (Cardinals). Massive 2025 disappointment. Sat 94-96 MPH down from 97/98. Sinker/sweeper foundation needs better location. STL great landing spot — good defense, pitcher's park, long leash. Sleeper in 15-teamers.

SP #103 (Dodgers). Two-pitch arm (FB/splitter) w/ poor slider. 96 MPH four-seam had just 5.5% SwStr across 300+ thrown. Splitter and slider both fail as strike pitches. Too much to fix for 12-teamer relevance.
RP (Marlins). Power arm with elite velocity. Closing experience. SV+HLD value when healthy. Late-round reliever with save upside.
